prompt: Implement a GreenLeaf Operations Suite for an offline-first plant-care retailer and greenhouse operator, covering day-to-day procurement plus internal knowledge sharing in one React-based web UI. Users sign in with a locally managed username and password and land on a role-based workspace for Procurement Managers, Warehouse Clerks, Plant Care Specialists, and Administrators; suppliers can be given limited portal access on shared workstations for viewing purchase orders and return authorizations. The procurement flow supports purchase-demand creation from low-stock alerts or manual requests, an approval chain with dollar thresholds (auto-approve up to $500.00, supervisor approval up to $5,000.00, and dual approval beyond that), and a closed loop from approved request to purchase order, receiving, and putaway. Receiving screens guide barcode/manual entry, require variance reason codes for short/over/damaged items, and generate returns with a default 14-day return window and configurable restocking fee rules. A Plant Care knowledge base provides structured articles (care guides, pest treatment SOPs, safety notes) with version history, change summaries, favorites, and offline cache on the device; Administrators can run phased releases so drafts are visible only to Specialists until promoted to “Storewide.” Intelligent search supports tags, synonyms, “similar articles” suggestions, and saved search history. A unified in-app notification center surfaces event-driven messages such as request approval, schedule changes, cancellations, and review outcomes, with read receipts, per-user subscription preferences, and throttling (no more than 20 notifications per user per hour) to prevent alert fatigue. The backend uses NestJS to expose RESTful APIs consumed by the React frontend, enforcing server-side validation, role permissions, and transactional integrity, with PostgreSQL as the system of record for suppliers, payment terms, requests, purchase orders, receipts, putaway locations, knowledge articles, notifications, and audit trails. Supplier profiles store payment terms (for example Net 30, 2/10 Net 30) and maintain a local funds ledger for recording deposits, escrow holds, and refunds as accounting events; third-party payment connectors remain pluggable but are disabled by default to keep the system fully functional without internet access, and all callbacks are handled idempotently when enabled in a controlled on-prem environment. Entity deduplication and ID alignment prevent duplicate catalog and knowledge records by generating fingerprints (normalized title, supplier, unit size, and optional UPC) and applying fuzzy matching thresholds (flag matches at 0.90 similarity for review, auto-merge at 0.97 with admin approval), maintaining mapping tables so historical reports and training datasets aren’t contaminated by duplicates. Data quality and observability run locally: scheduled integrity checks detect missing required fields, duplicate suppliers, and outlier pricing (over 3× median), while trace logs, job duration metrics, queue length, and resource usage are available in an admin dashboard; failed jobs retry up to three times with exponential backoff and automatically roll back partial writes via database transactions. Sensitive fields (password hashes, supplier banking notes, internal risk flags) are encrypted or masked at rest, passwords are hashed with bcrypt, sessions expire after 30 minutes of inactivity, and the entire deployment is designed to run on a single machine or local LAN with no external dependencies.
